dublinCore.dc:title: 114 S4102 IS: Delaware River Basin Restoration Program Reauthorization Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Ms. Blunt Rochester (for herself, Mr. Schumer, Mrs. Gillibrand, Mr. Booker, Mr. Kim, Mr. Coons, Mr. Fetterman, Ms. Alsobrooks, and Mr. Van Hollen) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Environment and Public Works

form.official-title: To amend the Water Infrastructure Improvements for the Nation Act to reauthorize Delaware River Basin conservation programs, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Delaware River Basin Restoration Program Reauthorization Act of 2026.

subsection.text: Section 3501(2) of the Water Infrastructure Improvements for the Nation Act (Public Law 114–322; 130 Stat. 1771) is amended by inserting Maryland, after Delaware,.

subsection.header: Definitions of Basin and Basin State
subsection.text: Section 3502 of the Water Infrastructure Improvements for the Nation Act (Public Law 114–322; 130 Stat. 1773) is amended—

subparagraph.text: by inserting Maryland, after Delaware,; and

paragraph.text: in paragraph (2), by inserting Maryland, after Delaware,.

subsection.text: Section 3504 of the Water Infrastructure Improvements for the Nation Act (Public Law 114–322; 130 Stat. 1774) is amended by adding at the end the following:
In selecting a project to be funded under the grant program, the Secretary may give priority to a project that serves a small, rural, or disadvantaged community.
Section 3507 of the Water Infrastructure Improvements for the Nation Act (Public Law 114–322; 130 Stat. 1775) is amended by striking 2023 and inserting 2033.
